user_prompt:
User Requirement: do a shallow water simulation over a square bump using the shallowWaterFoam solver. The domain spans 1 x 1 x 0.1, discretized into 20 x 20 x 1 grid points. The simulation initializes with a uniform water depth of 0.02 m and a uniform momentum field of (0.001,0,0). The simulation runs from 0 s to 100 s with a timestep of 0.1 s and writes results every 1 s. 

Reference Directory Structure (similar case): <dir>directory name: 0. File names in this directory: [h.orig, hTotal, hU.orig]</dir>
<dir>directory name: system. File names in this directory: [blockMeshDict, controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution, setFieldsDict]</dir>
<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [gravitationalProperties, h0.orig]</dir>

There are 3 files in Directory: 0,
There are 5 files in Directory: system,
There are 2 files in Directory: constant

Make sure you generate all the necessary files for the user's requirements.Do not include any gmsh files like .geo etc. in the subtasks.Only include blockMesh or snappyHexMesh if the user hasnt requested for gmsh mesh or user isnt using an external uploaded custom meshPlease generate the output as structured JSON.
response: subtasks=[SubtaskModel(file_name='h.orig', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='hTotal', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='hU.orig', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='setFieldsDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='gravitationalProperties', folder_name='constant'), SubtaskModel(file_name='h0.orig', folder_name='constant')]
